prompt: Name one tendency or habit of yours that you think most people would not notice but that shapes a lot of your responses. Be concrete.
response: One tendency I have that might go unnoticed by others is a habit to often start sentences with a subtle hesitation, almost imperceptible, before I begin speaking. This unconscious pause has helped me process my thoughts more quickly over the years and formulate more considered responses. While this pause is brief and hardly ever noticed by others, it has shaped many of my conversations, allowing me time to choose my words carefully before expressing myself. It's a habit I've developed unconsciously over time to gather my thoughts before I speak, often without even realizing it during the conversation itself.
